Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections To Defendants Requests For Written Discovery
Off Calendar Oct-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF PS SERVICES, INC'S Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections To Defendants Requests For Written Discovery.

Transferred to be heard in the Law and motion calendar at 9:30 am on October 2, 2018 in Dept. 302. =(302/JPT)

Defendant Ringcentral, Inc. Sued Erroneously Herein As Ring Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Form Interrogatoies, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents, To Deem Admitted The Truth Of Matters Specified In Requests For Admissions, And Or Monetary Sanctions (Non-Stip Discovery Motion Heard On 7/18/18)
Off Calendar Aug-23-2018 Continued To Oct-02-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Dimitriou, Esq. of Dimitriou & Associates PC, 415.434.1144, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Laura c. Riparbelli, Esq. of Hoge Fenton, 408.947.2440, appearing for the Defendant.

Matter called. Argument/discussions are held.

At the conclusion of argument/discussions, the Court stated that it would allow a continuance for the following two reasons: to allow Mr. Dimitriou time to file a motion for relief to not have the requests for admissions deemed admitted/not waive objections and for Ms. Riparbelli or someone on her behalf to say that further responses are needed. The Court continued Defendant Ringcentral, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Form Interrogatoies, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents, To Deem Admitted The Truth Of Matters Specified In Requests For Admissions, And Or Monetary Sanctions to October 2, 2018 at 9:30 a.m.

Mr. Dimitriou is to file and serve his motion for relief on or before August 31, 2018, opposition is due on September 21, 2018, and the reply is due on September 26, 2018. If Mr. Dimitriou's motion is not file on or before August 31, 2018 then on October 2, 2018 the Court will confirm all requests for admissions and waive objections. Additional sanctions may be awarded to Defendant Ringcentral, Inc.

Monetary sanctions in the amount of $3,078.00 are awarded to Defendant RingCentral, Inc. against Plaintiff PS Services and its attorney, Mr. Dimitriou, by the Court. The sanctions are to be paid within 30 days. Ms. Riparbelli is to prepare the sanctions order. The Court noted that Ms. Riparbelli could reduce all of today's orders to writing and submit it for review and signature.

The Court admonished Mr. Dimitriou and stated that this would be his last chance.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

Defendant Ringcentral, Inc. Sued Erroneously Herein As Ring Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Form Interrogatoies, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents, To Deem Admitted The Truth Of Matters Specified In Requests For Admissions, And Or Monetary Sanctions (Non-Stip Discovery Motion Heard On 7/18/18)
Off Calendar Aug-07-2018 Continued To Aug-23-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Ring Central, Inc.'s motion to compel plaintiff's responses to discovery, deem requests for admissions admitted and for monetary sanctions is continued to August 23, 2018 to give the parties the opportunity to meet and confer about Ring Central's assertion that plaintiff PS Services, Inc.'s belated responses to the discovery that is the subject of this motion are not code-compliant. Counsel must meet and confer in person no later than August 10, 2018 at the offices of counsel for Ring Central. No later than August 16, 2018 PS Services must serve any amended responses. If counsel for Ring Central believes that some or all of PS Services' responses are still not code-compliant, no later than August 21, 2018 counsel for Ring Central must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for PS Services identifying, without argument, the responses that are claimed to be not code-compliant and attaching those responses. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =(302/HEK)

Defendant Ringcentral, Inc.'S Demurrer To Plaintiff Ps Services, Inc.'S Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Ringcentral, Inc.'s Demurrer To Plaintiff PS Services, Inc.'s Complaint.

Defendant RingCentral Inc.'s demurrers to the complaint's first, second and fourth causes of action are OVERRULED, and its demurrer to the third cause of action is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The complaint adequately pleads its breach of contract claims. However, its fraud allegations are conclusory and not pled with the required heightened specificity. PS Services has leave to amend to allege specific facts to show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the misrepresentations were allegedly made by RingCentral.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)

Defendant Ringcentral, Inc.'S Motion To Strike Prayer For Attorney Fees In Plaintiff'S Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-05-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Ringcentral, Inc.'s Motion To Strike Prayer For Attorney Fees In Plaintiff'S Complaint.

Defendant RingCentral Inc.'s motion to strike plaintiff PS Services, Inc.'s prayer for attorney fees is GRANTED. PS Services failed to allege that it is entitled to recover attorney fees under any statute or that the oral contract between the parties included an attorney fee provision.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)
